articles.headline: Government Launches Ambitious 'National Urban Greening Mission' to Combat Climate Change and Enhance Urban Ecology
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, in collaboration with the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, launched the 'National Urban Greening Mission' (NUGM) with a vision to transform Indian cities into sustainable, green, and resilient urban ecosystems.
• NUGM aims to significantly increase urban green cover, improve air quality, mitigate urban heat island effect, and enhance biodiversity within cities through a multi-pronged strategy.
• Key initiatives under the mission include large-scale tree plantation drives, development of urban forests using Miyawaki technique, restoration of urban wetlands and water bodies, and promotion of green infrastructure.
• The mission emphasizes public-private partnerships, community participation, and leveraging technology for monitoring and management of green spaces.
• Funding will be mobilized through a combination of central and state grants, municipal bonds, and corporate social responsibility (CSR) initiatives, with a target to achieve 30% green cover in major cities by 2030.

Environmental impact assessment
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies

mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted benefits of urban greening for environmental sustainability, public health, and climate resilience.
Analyze the role of inter-ministerial coordination and public-private partnerships in successful implementation of large-scale urban development projects.
Evaluate NUGM's potential to address issues like air pollution, urban flooding, and biodiversity loss in Indian cities.
Examine the challenges in achieving urban greening targets, including land availability, funding, and maintenance.
articles.imageDescription: A lush urban park with walking paths and dense tree cover, representing a successful urban greening initiative.
articles.trivia: The Miyawaki method, pioneered by Japanese botanist Akira Miyawaki, involves planting native species close together to encourage rapid, dense forest growth.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Crucial Final Phase, Optimism for Early Conclusion
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the ambitious India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their final crucial phase, with both sides expressing optimism for an early conclusion.
• The 7th round of talks, held in Brussels, saw significant progress on key contentious areas including market access for goods, services, investment protection, and geographical indications.
• An FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, reduce tariffs, and create new opportunities for businesses in both regions.
• India is keen on greater access for its textiles, pharmaceuticals, and agricultural products, while the EU seeks reduced duties on automobiles, wines, and dairy products.
• The agreement is seen as a strategic move to diversify supply chains and strengthen economic ties amidst evolving global geopolitical landscapes.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing Attracts Significant Investment, Boosting EV Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage has seen remarkable success, with major domestic and international players committing significant investments in India.
• This surge in investment is crucial for establishing a robust local manufacturing ecosystem for electric vehicle (EV) batteries, reducing dependence on imports.
• The scheme aims to achieve a manufacturing capacity of 50 GWh of ACC batteries, fostering self-reliance and making India a global hub for EV battery production.
• This development is expected to drive down battery costs, accelerate EV adoption, and create numerous job opportunities in the green mobility sector.
• The government is also working on supportive policies for raw material sourcing and research & development to sustain this growth.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Core Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, with Clarifications
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of the core provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, dismissing several petitions challenging its framework.
• The Court, however, issued specific clarifications regarding the scope of 'significant data fiduciaries' and the necessity principle for data processing by state entities.
• This landmark judgment provides much-needed legal certainty for businesses and individuals regarding data privacy and protection in the digital age.
• The DPDP Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process personal data for lawful purposes.
• Experts believe the ruling will accelerate the implementation of the Act and establish a robust data governance framework in India.

articles.headline: India Hosts 3rd 'No Money For Terror' Ministerial Conference, Reinforcing Global Cooperation Against Terror Financing
articles.explainer:
• India is hosting the 3rd 'No Money For Terror' (NMFT) Ministerial Conference in New Delhi, bringing together global leaders and experts to deliberate on combating terror financing.
• The conference focuses on four key themes: Global trends in terrorism and terror financing, formal and informal channels of terror financing, emerging technologies and terror financing, and international cooperation to address terror financing.
• This initiative underscores India's commitment to strengthening international cooperation and intelligence sharing to choke funding sources for terrorist organizations.
• Discussions include strategies to counter the use of new financial technologies, virtual assets, and crowdfunding for illicit purposes by terror groups.
• The outcomes of the conference are expected to contribute to a unified global approach and enhanced actionable strategies against terror financing networks.

articles.headline: Government Expands PM-KISAN Scope, Introduces Incentives for Climate-Resilient Farming Practices
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has approved an expansion of the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme, increasing its outreach to include more marginal farmers previously excluded due to certain landholding criteria.
• A significant new component introduced is a direct incentive for farmers adopting verified climate-resilient farming practices, such as natural farming, crop diversification, and efficient water management techniques.
• This move aims to bolster farmers' income while simultaneously promoting sustainable agriculture and mitigating the impacts of climate change on the agricultural sector.
• The expanded scheme also streamlines the beneficiary verification process using Aadhaar and digital land records to ensure transparency and prevent leakages.
• This initiative aligns with India's commitments to climate action and food security, recognizing farmers as key stakeholders in environmental conservation.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches 'OceanSat-4' Satellite for Advanced Oceanographic Studies and Climate Monitoring
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched 'OceanSat-4', an advanced earth observation satellite dedicated to oceanographic studies and climate monitoring.
• Launched aboard the PSLV-C58 rocket from Sriharikota, OceanSat-4 carries enhanced sensors for measuring sea surface temperature, ocean color, wind vectors, and monitoring marine pollution.
• This mission is critical for improving weather forecasting, understanding ocean dynamics, and supporting coastal zone management and fisheries.
• The data from OceanSat-4 will significantly contribute to global climate research and provide vital inputs for disaster management and marine resource planning.
• It marks another milestone in India's growing capabilities in space-based observation for environmental and scientific applications.

articles.headline: New 'National Wetland Conservation Programme' Launched with Focus on Community Participation and Sustainable Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched a revamped 'National Wetland Conservation Programme' (NWCP), emphasizing a holistic approach to wetland management.
• The new programme prioritizes community participation, integration of traditional knowledge, and sustainable livelihood options for local communities dependent on wetlands.
• It aims to identify and restore critically degraded wetlands, enhance their ecological services, and raise public awareness about their importance.
• Special focus will be given to Ramsar Sites and other wetlands of national importance, ensuring their ecological character is maintained through scientific management plans.
• The initiative seeks to strengthen India's commitment to the Ramsar Convention and contribute to global biodiversity conservation goals.

articles.headline: Election Commission Unveils 'Digital Voter ID' Pilot Project in Select Urban Constituencies
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) has launched a pilot project for a 'Digital Voter ID' in select urban constituencies, aiming to enhance voter accessibility and streamline the electoral process.
• This initiative allows voters to access their Electors Photo Identity Card (EPIC) digitally through a secure mobile application or web portal, alongside the physical card.
• The digital ID incorporates advanced security features, including QR codes and encrypted personal data, to prevent forgery and ensure authentication at polling booths.
• The pilot project will assess the technological feasibility, security aspects, and public acceptance before a potential nationwide rollout.
• This move is part of ECI's broader efforts towards leveraging technology for more efficient, transparent, and inclusive elections.

articles.headline: National Infrastructure Pipeline Progress Report Highlights Significant Strides in Transport and Energy Sectors
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance today released a progress report on the National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP), showcasing significant advancements in project implementation across various sectors.
• The report highlights substantial investment and completion rates in key sectors such as roads, railways, ports, airports, and renewable energy.
• NIP, launched in 2019, aims to provide world-class infrastructure across the country, improving the quality of life for all citizens, and is crucial for India's economic growth trajectory.
• Public-private partnerships (PPPs) have played a vital role in mobilizing funds and expertise, accelerating project execution.
• The government reiterates its commitment to further accelerate infrastructure development to achieve the vision of a $5 trillion economy.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Milestone with Over 50 Crore Health IDs Generated
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has achieved a significant milestone, successfully generating over 50 crore Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) across the country.
• This achievement marks a major step towards creating a seamless online platform for healthcare, enabling individuals to access and share their health records digitally with their consent.
• ABDM aims to bridge the existing gap among different stakeholders of the healthcare ecosystem through digital highways, facilitating interoperability of health data.
• The mission includes components like Healthcare Professionals Registry (HPR), Health Facility Registry (HFR), and Ayushman Bharat Health Records App.
• This digital transformation is expected to enhance access to quality healthcare, improve efficiency, and foster a more patient-centric health system in India.

articles.headline: India-Africa Summit Focuses on Enhancing Trade, Investment, and Capacity Building, with Emphasis on Renewable Energy
articles.explainer:
• The biennial India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming strong ties and outlining a roadmap for enhanced cooperation across diverse sectors.
• A key focus of the summit was boosting trade and investment, with India offering Lines of Credit for various developmental projects in African nations.
• Significant emphasis was placed on renewable energy cooperation, with India pledging support for solar energy projects and capacity building under the International Solar Alliance (ISA) framework.
• Discussions also covered critical areas like food security, health, education, and digital transformation, aiming for a partnership based on equality and mutual benefit.
• The summit underscored India's commitment to being a reliable development partner for Africa, contributing to its sustainable growth and prosperity.

articles.headline: DRDO Successfully Tests Advanced Version of Indigenous Anti-Tank Guided Missile (ATGM)
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) today successfully conducted the flight test of an advanced version of its indigenous Anti-Tank Guided Missile (ATGM) from the Pokhran field firing ranges.
• The missile demonstrated enhanced range and improved accuracy, capable of engaging targets with high precision under various operational conditions.
• This development significantly boosts the 'Make in India' initiative in the defense sector and strengthens the Indian Army's anti-tank warfare capabilities.
• The advanced ATGM features a new generation imaging infrared seeker and advanced guidance algorithms, making it highly effective against modern armored threats.
• Further trials are planned for integration with various launch platforms, including infantry, mechanized infantry, and helicopter-borne systems.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Scheme for Promotion and Preservation of Endangered Tribal Languages and Oral Traditions
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs, in collaboration with the Ministry of Culture, has launched a new comprehensive scheme aimed at the promotion and preservation of endangered tribal languages and their rich oral traditions.
• The scheme includes initiatives such as documentation, digitization, and development of educational materials in tribal languages, along with training programs for tribal youth as language custodians.
• Recognizing the critical role of language in cultural identity, the program seeks to prevent the extinction of vulnerable tribal languages and safeguard their unique cultural heritage.
• Partnerships with academic institutions, linguistic experts, and tribal communities will be central to the successful implementation of the scheme.
• This move underscores the government's commitment to protecting India's linguistic diversity and promoting the cultural rights of indigenous communities.

articles.headline: RBI's Pilot Project for Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) Expands to Include More Retail Transactions
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced the expansion of its pilot project for the Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), or e-Rupee, to include a wider range of retail transactions and participants.
• This expansion aims to test the CBDC's scalability, operational efficiency, and user acceptance in a more diverse real-world environment.
• The e-Rupee is designed to provide a digital alternative to physical cash, offering benefits like reduced transaction costs, faster settlements, and enhanced financial inclusion.
• New financial institutions and a larger user base will now participate in the retail CBDC pilot, enabling a comprehensive evaluation of its implications for monetary policy and the financial system.
• The move signals RBI's cautious yet progressive approach towards integrating digital currencies into India's financial landscape.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Releases New Guidelines for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) for Plastic Packaging
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF&CC) has released updated and more stringent guidelines for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) on plastic packaging.
• These new guidelines aim to enhance circularity in plastic waste management by holding producers, importers, and brand-owners more accountable for the entire lifecycle of their plastic products.
• Key features include mandatory recycling targets, use of recycled content, digital tracking of plastic waste, and provisions for incentivizing formal recycling channels.
• The revised framework seeks to curb plastic pollution, promote sustainable consumption and production patterns, and strengthen the informal waste collection sector.
• Industry stakeholders are urged to comply with the new norms, which are designed to push India towards a more sustainable and resource-efficient economy.

articles.headline: Inter-State Council Meeting Discusses Cooperative Federalism in Resource Sharing and Disaster Management
articles.explainer:
• The Inter-State Council held a crucial meeting today, chaired by the Prime Minister, to deliberate on key aspects of cooperative federalism, focusing particularly on resource sharing mechanisms and strengthening disaster management protocols.
• Chief Ministers and Union Ministers participated in extensive discussions on fiscal federalism, the distribution of tax revenues, and grants-in-aid to states, aiming for equitable development.
• A significant agenda point was the review of existing disaster management frameworks, proposing enhanced coordination between central and state agencies for preparedness, response, and rehabilitation.
• The Council emphasized the need for a collaborative approach to policy-making and implementation, respecting the autonomy of states while working towards national goals.
• Recommendations from the meeting are expected to be presented to the Union Government for further action, fostering a stronger federal structure.

articles.headline: PM-Vishwakarma Scheme Empowers Artisans and Craftsmen, Boosting Traditional Skills and Entrepreneurship
articles.explainer:
• The PM-Vishwakarma scheme, launched recently, has shown promising results in empowering traditional artisans and craftsmen across the country, providing them with financial support, skill training, and access to modern tools.
• The scheme aims to preserve and promote India's rich heritage of traditional crafts by integrating Vishwakarmas into the formal economy and enhancing their market reach.
• Beneficiaries receive collateral-free loans, skill upgradation training, a toolkit incentive, and support for digital transactions and marketing.
• This initiative is crucial for creating self-employment opportunities, particularly in rural and semi-urban areas, and fostering a sense of dignity for manual labor.
• The government plans to expand the scheme's outreach and include more traditional trades to further strengthen the 'Vocal for Local' movement.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress and Future Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting significant progress in foundational literacy and numeracy, curricular reforms, and digital education initiatives.
• The report notes successful integration of vocational education, increased multilingualism in early childhood education, and advancements in teacher training programs.
• However, challenges remain in achieving universal access to quality education, bridging the digital divide in remote areas, and ensuring adequate infrastructure in higher education institutions.
• The review emphasizes the need for continued collaboration between central and state governments, academic institutions, and civil society for holistic implementation.
• The NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century and position India as a global knowledge superpower.

articles.headline: UN Report Highlights Growing Global Climate Finance Gap, Urges Developed Nations to Meet Commitments
articles.explainer:
• A new report released by the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) warns of a widening global climate finance gap, underscoring the urgent need for developed nations to fulfill their commitments.
• The report indicates that the annual $100 billion climate finance goal, set for 2020, remains unmet, severely impacting climate adaptation and mitigation efforts in developing countries.
• It highlights the disproportionate burden faced by developing nations in combating climate change, despite their historically lower contributions to greenhouse gas emissions.
• The findings come ahead of the upcoming COP30 summit, putting pressure on developed economies to scale up financial and technological support for climate action.
• The report calls for innovative financing mechanisms, increased transparency, and a re-evaluation of climate finance targets to reflect current needs and scientific consensus.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'National Urban Greening Mission' (NUGM):
1. It is jointly launched by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs and the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements best describes the purpose of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing?
(a) To encourage the import of ACC batteries for renewable energy storage.
(b) To establish India as a global hub for traditional lead-acid battery manufacturing.
(c) To boost domestic manufacturing capacity of ACC batteries for electric vehicles and grid storage, reducing import dependence.
(d) To provide subsidies for consumers buying electric vehicles equipped with imported batteries.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To boost domestic manufacturing capacity of ACC batteries for electric vehicles and grid storage, reducing import dependence.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The PLI Scheme for ACC Battery Manufacturing aims to foster self-reliance in the EV battery ecosystem by incentivizing domestic production, thereby reducing reliance on imports and establishing India as a manufacturing hub.

prelimsMocks.question:

GS1.headline: Government Announces New Scheme for Promotion and Preservation of Endangered Tribal Languages and Oral Traditions
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs, in collaboration with the Ministry of Culture, has launched a new comprehensive scheme aimed at the promotion and preservation of endangered tribal languages and their rich oral traditions.
• The scheme includes initiatives such as documentation, digitization, and development of educational materials in tribal languages, along with training programs for tribal youth as language custodians.
• Recognizing the critical role of language in cultural identity, the program seeks to prevent the extinction of vulnerable tribal languages and safeguard their unique cultural heritage.
• Partnerships with academic institutions, linguistic experts, and tribal communities will be central to the successful implementation of the scheme.
• This move underscores the government's commitment to protecting India's linguistic diversity and promoting the cultural rights of indigenous communities.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Crucial Final Phase, Optimism for Early Conclusion
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the ambitious India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their final crucial phase, with both sides expressing optimism for an early conclusion.
• The 7th round of talks, held in Brussels, saw significant progress on key contentious areas including market access for goods, services, investment protection, and geographical indications.
• An FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, reduce tariffs, and create new opportunities for businesses in both regions.
• India is keen on greater access for its textiles, pharmaceuticals, and agricultural products, while the EU seeks reduced duties on automobiles, wines, and dairy products.
• The agreement is seen as a strategic move to diversify supply chains and strengthen economic ties amidst evolving global geopolitical landscapes.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Core Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, with Clarifications
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of the core provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, dismissing several petitions challenging its framework.
• The Court, however, issued specific clarifications regarding the scope of 'significant data fiduciaries' and the necessity principle for data processing by state entities.
• This landmark judgment provides much-needed legal certainty for businesses and individuals regarding data privacy and protection in the digital age.
• The DPDP Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process personal data for lawful purposes.
• Experts believe the ruling will accelerate the implementation of the Act and establish a robust data governance framework in India.

GS2.headline: Election Commission Unveils 'Digital Voter ID' Pilot Project in Select Urban Constituencies
GS2.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) has launched a pilot project for a 'Digital Voter ID' in select urban constituencies, aiming to enhance voter accessibility and streamline the electoral process.
• This initiative allows voters to access their Electors Photo Identity Card (EPIC) digitally through a secure mobile application or web portal, alongside the physical card.
• The digital ID incorporates advanced security features, including QR codes and encrypted personal data, to prevent forgery and ensure authentication at polling booths.
• The pilot project will assess the technological feasibility, security aspects, and public acceptance before a potential nationwide rollout.
• This move is part of ECI's broader efforts towards leveraging technology for more efficient, transparent, and inclusive elections.

GS3.headline: Government Launches Ambitious 'National Urban Greening Mission' to Combat Climate Change and Enhance Urban Ecology
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, in collaboration with the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, launched the 'National Urban Greening Mission' (NUGM) with a vision to transform Indian cities into sustainable, green, and resilient urban ecosystems.
• NUGM aims to significantly increase urban green cover, improve air quality, mitigate urban heat island effect, and enhance biodiversity within cities through a multi-pronged strategy.
• Key initiatives under the mission include large-scale tree plantation drives, development of urban forests using Miyawaki technique, restoration of urban wetlands and water bodies, and promotion of green infrastructure.
• The mission emphasizes public-private partnerships, community participation, and leveraging technology for monitoring and management of green spaces.
• Funding will be mobilized through a combination of central and state grants, municipal bonds, and corporate social responsibility (CSR) initiatives, with a target to achieve 30% green cover in major cities by 2030.

GS3.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing Attracts Significant Investment, Boosting EV Ecosystem
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage has seen remarkable success, with major domestic and international players committing significant investments in India.
• This surge in investment is crucial for establishing a robust local manufacturing ecosystem for electric vehicle (EV) batteries, reducing dependence on imports.
• The scheme aims to achieve a manufacturing capacity of 50 GWh of ACC batteries, fostering self-reliance and making India a global hub for EV battery production.
• This development is expected to drive down battery costs, accelerate EV adoption, and create numerous job opportunities in the green mobility sector.
• The government is also working on supportive policies for raw material sourcing and research & development to sustain this growth.

GS3.headline: India Hosts 3rd 'No Money For Terror' Ministerial Conference, Reinforcing Global Cooperation Against Terror Financing
GS3.explainer:
• India is hosting the 3rd 'No Money For Terror' (NMFT) Ministerial Conference in New Delhi, bringing together global leaders and experts to deliberate on combating terror financing.
• The conference focuses on four key themes: Global trends in terrorism and terror financing, formal and informal channels of terror financing, emerging technologies and terror financing, and international cooperation to address terror financing.
• This initiative underscores India's commitment to strengthening international cooperation and intelligence sharing to choke funding sources for terrorist organizations.
• Discussions include strategies to counter the use of new financial technologies, virtual assets, and crowdfunding for illicit purposes by terror groups.
• The outcomes of the conference are expected to contribute to a unified global approach and enhanced actionable strategies against terror financing networks.

GS3.headline: Government Expands PM-KISAN Scope, Introduces Incentives for Climate-Resilient Farming Practices
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has approved an expansion of the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme, increasing its outreach to include more marginal farmers previously excluded due to certain landholding criteria.
• A significant new component introduced is a direct incentive for farmers adopting verified climate-resilient farming practices, such as natural farming, crop diversification, and efficient water management techniques.
• This move aims to bolster farmers' income while simultaneously promoting sustainable agriculture and mitigating the impacts of climate change on the agricultural sector.
• The expanded scheme also streamlines the beneficiary verification process using Aadhaar and digital land records to ensure transparency and prevent leakages.
• This initiative aligns with India's commitments to climate action and food security, recognizing farmers as key stakeholders in environmental conservation.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches 'OceanSat-4' Satellite for Advanced Oceanographic Studies and Climate Monitoring
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched 'OceanSat-4', an advanced earth observation satellite dedicated to oceanographic studies and climate monitoring.
• Launched aboard the PSLV-C58 rocket from Sriharikota, OceanSat-4 carries enhanced sensors for measuring sea surface temperature, ocean color, wind vectors, and monitoring marine pollution.
• This mission is critical for improving weather forecasting, understanding ocean dynamics, and supporting coastal zone management and fisheries.
• The data from OceanSat-4 will significantly contribute to global climate research and provide vital inputs for disaster management and marine resource planning.
• It marks another milestone in India's growing capabilities in space-based observation for environmental and scientific applications.

GS3.headline: New 'National Wetland Conservation Programme' Launched with Focus on Community Participation and Sustainable Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched a revamped 'National Wetland Conservation Programme' (NWCP), emphasizing a holistic approach to wetland management.
• The new programme prioritizes community participation, integration of traditional knowledge, and sustainable livelihood options for local communities dependent on wetlands.
• It aims to identify and restore critically degraded wetlands, enhance their ecological services, and raise public awareness about their importance.
• Special focus will be given to Ramsar Sites and other wetlands of national importance, ensuring their ecological character is maintained through scientific management plans.
• The initiative seeks to strengthen India's commitment to the Ramsar Convention and contribute to global biodiversity conservation goals.

GS3.trivia: India has over 75 Ramsar Sites, which are wetlands of international importance designated under the Ramsar Convention.

GS3.headline: National Infrastructure Pipeline Progress Report Highlights Significant Strides in Transport and Energy Sectors
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance today released a progress report on the National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP), showcasing significant advancements in project implementation across various sectors.
• The report highlights substantial investment and completion rates in key sectors such as roads, railways, ports, airports, and renewable energy.
• NIP, launched in 2019, aims to provide world-class infrastructure across the country, improving the quality of life for all citizens, and is crucial for India's economic growth trajectory.
• Public-private partnerships (PPPs) have played a vital role in mobilizing funds and expertise, accelerating project execution.
• The government reiterates its commitment to further accelerate infrastructure development to achieve the vision of a $5 trillion economy.

GS3.headline: DRDO Successfully Tests Advanced Version of Indigenous Anti-Tank Guided Missile (ATGM)
GS3.explainer:
• The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) today successfully conducted the flight test of an advanced version of its indigenous Anti-Tank Guided Missile (ATGM) from the Pokhran field firing ranges.
• The missile demonstrated enhanced range and improved accuracy, capable of engaging targets with high precision under various operational conditions.
• This development significantly boosts the 'Make in India' initiative in the defense sector and strengthens the Indian Army's anti-tank warfare capabilities.
• The advanced ATGM features a new generation imaging infrared seeker and advanced guidance algorithms, making it highly effective against modern armored threats.
• Further trials are planned for integration with various launch platforms, including infantry, mechanized infantry, and helicopter-borne systems.

GS3.headline: RBI's Pilot Project for Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) Expands to Include More Retail Transactions
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced the expansion of its pilot project for the Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), or e-Rupee, to include a wider range of retail transactions and participants.
• This expansion aims to test the CBDC's scalability, operational efficiency, and user acceptance in a more diverse real-world environment.
• The e-Rupee is designed to provide a digital alternative to physical cash, offering benefits like reduced transaction costs, faster settlements, and enhanced financial inclusion.
• New financial institutions and a larger user base will now participate in the retail CBDC pilot, enabling a comprehensive evaluation of its implications for monetary policy and the financial system.
• The move signals RBI's cautious yet progressive approach towards integrating digital currencies into India's financial landscape.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Environment Releases New Guidelines for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) for Plastic Packaging
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF&CC) has released updated and more stringent guidelines for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) on plastic packaging.
• These new guidelines aim to enhance circularity in plastic waste management by holding producers, importers, and brand-owners more accountable for the entire lifecycle of their plastic products.
• Key features include mandatory recycling targets, use of recycled content, digital tracking of plastic waste, and provisions for incentivizing formal recycling channels.
• The revised framework seeks to curb plastic pollution, promote sustainable consumption and production patterns, and strengthen the informal waste collection sector.
• Industry stakeholders are urged to comply with the new norms, which are designed to push India towards a more sustainable and resource-efficient economy.

GS3.headline: UN Report Highlights Growing Global Climate Finance Gap, Urges Developed Nations to Meet Commitments
GS3.explainer:
• A new report released by the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) warns of a widening global climate finance gap, underscoring the urgent need for developed nations to fulfill their commitments.
• The report indicates that the annual $100 billion climate finance goal, set for 2020, remains unmet, severely impacting climate adaptation and mitigation efforts in developing countries.
• It highlights the disproportionate burden faced by developing nations in combating climate change, despite their historically lower contributions to greenhouse gas emissions.
• The findings come ahead of the upcoming COP30 summit, putting pressure on developed economies to scale up financial and technological support for climate action.
• The report calls for innovative financing mechanisms, increased transparency, and a re-evaluation of climate finance targets to reflect current needs and scientific consensus.
